verdict.answer_first: A leads harder: “Heap snapshot diffing is the technique that actually finds it.” B also starts well: “fastest reliable path is diffing heap snapshots,” but A is more outcome-first.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to one prescription: “Everything else just confirms a leak exists” and “workflow I’d use, in order.” B adds more branches/options like “Allocation timeline,” “Raise the ceiling,” and “Auto-dump on OOM.”
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute immediately: “node --heapsnapshot-signal=SIGUSR2 your-app.js” plus “kill -USR2 <pid>” and a “Quick start.” B’s “node --inspect” path is less direct and includes a dangling “or trigger snapshots programmatically:” setup.
